# Beudox HR Management System - Technical Context

## Architecture Overview

Beudox HR is a modern React-based single-page application (SPA) built with TypeScript, following a component-driven architecture. The application implements a multi-tenant SaaS model using Supabase as the backend platform.

### Application Architecture
- **Frontend**: React 18 with TypeScript, built with Vite
- **Routing**: React Router with protected routes and role-based access control
- **State Management**: React Query for server state, React hooks for local state
- **Styling**: Tailwind CSS with shadcn/ui component system
- **Backend**: Supabase (PostgreSQL + Auth + Storage + Edge Functions)
- **Deployment**: Static site hosting (Vercel/Netlify compatible)

### Key Architectural Patterns
- **Component Composition**: Extensive use of compound components and render props
- **Custom Hooks**: Business logic abstracted into reusable hooks
- **Provider Pattern**: Context providers for auth, theming, and global state
- **Protected Routes**: Route-level protection with role-based redirects
- **Multi-tenant RLS**: Database-level isolation using Row Level Security

## Core Components & Structure

### Layout System
- `AppLayout`: Main application shell with sidebar navigation
- `AppSidebar`: Collapsible navigation with role-based menu items
- `TopBar`: User menu, notifications, and global actions
- Responsive design with mobile-first approach

### Feature Components
- **Employee Management**: SearchableEmployeeSelect, EmployeeProfile components
- **Evaluations**: EvaluationTimeline, evaluation forms with scoring
- **Rich Text Editing**: TipTap-based editor for HR policies
- **Data Visualization**: Charts for financial and performance metrics
- **Form System**: React Hook Form with Zod validation throughout

### UI Component Library
- 40+ shadcn/ui components customized for the application
- Consistent design tokens and theme system
- Accessible components with proper ARIA labels
- Dark/light mode support via next-themes

## Database Schema & Relationships

### Core Entities

#### Companies
- Multi-tenant root entity
- Fields: id, name, slug, settings
- RLS: Users can only access their own company data

#### Employees
- Central user entity linked to Supabase Auth
- Fields: id, auth_user_id, company_id, full_name, email, employee_code, designation, department, avatar_url, status
- Relationships: belongs_to company, has_many roles through employee_roles
- RLS: Users can read their own record, managers can read team/company data

#### Roles & Permissions
- Roles table: id, company_id, name (ceo, hr_manager, finance_manager, team_lead, employee)
- Employee_roles junction table for many-to-many relationship
- Permission checking via `canAccess()` function with hardcoded rules

### Business Entities

#### Evaluations
- Quarterly evaluations: period, overall_score, comments, recommendation
- Daily evaluations: direction (positive/constructive), date, score, remarks
- Relationships: evaluator_id, reviewee_id, company_id
- Visibility rules: Managers see all, employees see their own, team leads see team data

#### Financial Tables
- Invoices: client_id, amount, status, PDF generation
- Payroll: employee_id, period, calculations (basic salary, allowances, overtime)
- Monthly expenses: categorized expense tracking
- Loans: employee loans with tracking

#### Organizational Tables
- Projects: name, description, team assignments
- Clients: company relationships
- HR Policies: rich text documents
- Public holidays: configurable calendar
- Departments: organizational structure

### Database Security
- **Row Level Security (RLS)** enabled on all tables
- **Security Definer Functions**: `get_employee_by_auth_id()`, `get_company_id_for_auth()`
- **Policy-based Access**: Different policies for different roles (ceo, hr_manager, finance_manager, etc.)
- **Company Isolation**: All queries filtered by company_id

## Business Logic & Data Flow

### Authentication Flow
1. User authenticates via Supabase Auth
2. `useAuth` hook fetches employee data via `get_employee_by_auth_id()`
3. Role permissions loaded and cached
4. Protected routes check `canAccess()` before rendering
5. Password reset flow for new invites

### Evaluation System
- **Quarterly Reviews**: Formal performance assessments with recommendations
- **Daily Feedback**: Real-time peer/team feedback with positive/constructive direction
- **Timeline View**: Chronological display with filtering based on user role
- **Scoring**: 1-5 star rating system
- **Visibility**: Role-based access (managers see recommendations, employees don't)

### Payroll Processing
- **Edge Function**: `generate-payroll` processes attendance data
- **Calculations**: Basic salary + allowances + overtime (regular + holiday)
- **PDF Generation**: Automated payslip creation
- **Approval Workflow**: Finance manager review process

### Invoice Management
- **CRUD Operations**: Create/edit invoices with line items
- **PDF Generation**: `generate-invoice-pdf` edge function
- **Email Delivery**: `send-invoice-email` with PDF attachments
- **Client Integration**: Linked to projects and clients

### File Upload & Storage
- **Supabase Storage**: Avatar images, policy documents, generated PDFs
- **Signed URLs**: Secure access to private files
- **Image Processing**: Client-side cropping for avatars

## Key Files & Their Purposes

### Application Entry Points
- `src/main.tsx`: React root rendering
- `src/App.tsx`: Main app component with routing and providers
- `src/pages/Index.tsx`: Legacy entry point (not actively used)

### Core Business Logic
- `src/hooks/useAuth.ts`: Authentication state management
- `src/lib/role-access.ts`: Permission checking logic
- `src/lib/utils.ts`: Utility functions (cn for class merging)
- `src/integrations/supabase/client.ts`: Supabase client configuration

### Component Architecture
- `src/components/layout/`: Layout system components
- `src/components/ui/`: Reusable UI primitives
- `src/components/[feature]/`: Feature-specific components
- `src/pages/`: Route-level page components

### Configuration Files
- `vite.config.ts`: Build configuration with path aliases
- `tailwind.config.ts`: Styling configuration
- `tsconfig.json`: TypeScript configuration
- `package.json`: Dependencies and scripts

### Supabase Integration
- `supabase/config.toml`: Supabase project configuration
- `supabase/migrations/`: Database schema evolution
- `supabase/functions/`: Server-side business logic

## Data Flow Patterns

### Query Patterns
- **React Query**: All API calls wrapped in useQuery/useMutation
- **Optimistic Updates**: Immediate UI feedback for mutations
- **Cache Invalidation**: Automatic refetching on related data changes
- **Error Handling**: Toast notifications for failures

### Form Patterns
- **React Hook Form**: Declarative form validation
- **Zod Schemas**: Runtime type validation
- **Controlled Components**: Consistent state management
- **File Uploads**: Drag-and-drop with preview

### Navigation Patterns
- **Protected Routes**: Authentication and authorization checks
- **Role-based Redirects**: Automatic redirection based on permissions
- **Breadcrumb Navigation**: Contextual navigation aids
- **Search/Filtering**: Client-side and server-side data filtering

## Performance Considerations

### Optimization Strategies
- **Code Splitting**: Route-based lazy loading
- **Image Optimization**: Next.js-style image component (planned)
- **Bundle Analysis**: Vite build analysis for optimization
- **Caching**: React Query caching with appropriate stale times
- **Virtual Scrolling**: For large lists (implemented in some components)

### Scalability Features
- **Multi-tenant**: Database-level isolation
- **Edge Functions**: Serverless compute for heavy operations
- **CDN Delivery**: Static asset optimization
- **Progressive Loading**: Skeleton screens and loading states

## Development Workflow

### Local Development
- `npm run dev`: Vite dev server on port 8080
- `npm run build`: Production build optimization
- `npm run test`: Vitest unit tests
- `npm run lint`: ESLint code quality checks

### Deployment
- Static site generation via Vite build
- Environment variables for different stages
- Supabase migrations applied via CLI
- CDN deployment (Vercel/Netlify)

This technical context provides the foundation for understanding the Beudox HR codebase and making informed decisions about modifications and extensions.
